description: /harden rule engine — 32 patterns extracted from scanning 2 real codebases across 7 pillars + deep scan

patterns.name: Duplicated security constant
patterns.description: Security-critical value (role list, allowed origins, etc.) hardcoded in multiple places instead of imported from single source of truth
patterns.detection: Grep for role arrays or security constants; cross-reference with canonical definitions. Look for string arrays containing role names.
patterns.fix: Extract to shared constant, import everywhere

patterns.name: Fire-and-forget async without .catch()
patterns.description: Promise chain (.then()) without .catch() handler — if the promise rejects, the error is silently swallowed or triggers unhandled rejection
patterns.detection: Grep for .then( without a following .catch(. Check signOut, API calls, and audit logging chains.
patterns.fix: Add .catch() with error logging. For critical paths (signOut), include fallback navigation.

patterns.name: Missing CSRF origin check on API route
patterns.description: POST/PUT/DELETE API route handler that does not validate the Origin header against the expected domain
patterns.detection: Check all API route handlers (app/api/*/route.ts) for Origin header validation before processing mutations
patterns.fix: Add Origin header check at the top of the handler: compare request Origin to expected origin, reject with 403 if mismatch

patterns.name: Cookie without secure flag
patterns.description: Cookie set without the secure flag, allowing transmission over unencrypted HTTP in production
patterns.detection: Grep for .cookies.set( and check for presence of secure: true or secure: process.env.NODE_ENV === 'production'
patterns.fix: Add secure: process.env.NODE_ENV === 'production' to cookie options

patterns.name: Wildcard CORS override in Firebase Functions
patterns.description: Firebase Functions v2 onRequest with `cors: true` in options sets Access-Control-Allow-Origin: * at SDK level before handler runs, bypassing any custom setCors() allowlist in the handler body
patterns.detection: Grep for `cors: true` in onRequest function options objects. Cross-reference with setCors() calls in the same handler body.
patterns.fix: Remove `cors: true` from options object. Rely on custom setCors() allowlist already in handler body.

patterns.name: Error details leaked in API response
patterns.description: Server error response includes raw error.message or stack trace, potentially exposing SMTP config, internal paths, or service details to clients
patterns.detection: Grep for `error.message` or `String(error)` inside NextResponse.json/res.json 500 responses. Check for 'details' field in error responses.
patterns.fix: Return only generic error message to client. Log detailed error server-side with console.error.

patterns.name: Unguarded signOut call
patterns.description: Firebase/Supabase signOut() call without try-catch — if sign-out fails (network issue, corrupted session), throws unhandled error that propagates to caller
patterns.detection: Grep for signOut( calls. Check if each is inside a try-catch or has a .catch() handler.
patterns.fix: Wrap in try-catch with console.error fallback. For critical paths, ensure user is still redirected to login.

patterns.name: Fail-open rate limiter
patterns.description: Rate limiter catch block returns allowed:true on database error — attacker can trigger storage errors to bypass rate limiting entirely
patterns.detection: Find rate limit functions. Check catch blocks — if they return allowed:true or equivalent, the limiter fails open.
patterns.fix: Change catch block to return allowed:false (fail-closed). Log the error for monitoring but deny the request.

patterns.name: Non-atomic idempotency check
patterns.description: Webhook/payment processing reads a document to check if already processed, then writes if not — without a transaction. Concurrent duplicate webhooks can both pass the check and process the payment twice (double loyalty points, double inventory deduction).
patterns.detection: Find webhook handlers or payment processors. Look for read-then-write patterns without runTransaction/atomicOperation wrapping.
patterns.fix: Wrap the check-and-set in a database transaction (Firestore runTransaction, Postgres SELECT FOR UPDATE). Only the first concurrent caller should succeed.

patterns.name: Client-only credential gate (server trigger ignores token)
patterns.description: Invitation/signup token validated only in the client UI, but the server-side trigger or backend function matches by email alone — anyone knowing the email can claim the intended role without possessing the token
patterns.detection: Trace the full lifecycle of tokens/credentials from UI to backend. Check if the backend function (DB trigger, Cloud Function) actually validates the token or just matches on a weaker identifier (email, userId).
patterns.fix: Pass the token to the backend. Validate it server-side before granting the role. Mark token as consumed atomically in the same transaction.

patterns.name: Non-atomic capacity reservation across payment boundary
patterns.description: Capacity/inventory check happens at payment initiation but slot is only reserved in the webhook after payment succeeds — concurrent requests can all pass the check, leading to overselling
patterns.detection: Trace checkout flow from capacity check to payment to webhook. Check if the capacity decrement is atomic with the check or if there is a window between check and reservation.
patterns.fix: Use optimistic locking: atomically decrement capacity at initiation time, refund the slot if payment fails or times out. Or use a reservation-with-TTL pattern.

patterns.name: Non-idempotent webhook handler
patterns.description: Payment webhook handler uses non-transactional increment operations (e.g., Firestore FieldValue.increment) that double-count if the webhook fires twice before the first write completes
patterns.detection: Check webhook handlers for idempotency guards. Look for increment() operations outside of transactions. Verify the idempotency check (e.g., order exists) is within the same transaction as the write.
patterns.fix: Wrap the entire webhook processing in a Firestore transaction. Check idempotency within the transaction. Use set-with-merge for the order, not increment for derived values.

patterns.name: Rate limiter with fail-open error path
patterns.description: Rate limiting function returns allowed:true when its backing store (Redis, Firestore, etc.) is unavailable — any transient DB error eliminates all rate limiting
patterns.detection: Read the catch block of the rate limiter. Check if it returns a permissive default (allowed: true) or a restrictive default (allowed: false).
patterns.fix: Change catch block to return allowed: false (fail-closed). Alternatively, use an in-memory fallback counter.

patterns.name: Overly permissive RLS policy
patterns.description: Development-time open RLS policy (WITH CHECK (true) for anon) coexists with production role-gated policies — the most permissive policy wins, bypassing role restrictions
patterns.detection: Grep for 'WITH CHECK (true)' or 'USING (true)' in RLS policies. Cross-reference with role-gated policies on same table. Flag if anon has write access.
patterns.fix: Drop dev-time open policies. Ensure only role-gated policies exist for write operations.

patterns.name: CSRF check that fails open
patterns.description: CSRF origin validation that skips the check when origin or host header is absent, rather than rejecting the request
patterns.detection: Check API route CSRF guards for `if (origin && host && ...)` pattern. The correct pattern is `if (!origin || origin !== expectedOrigin)` (fail closed).
patterns.fix: Change to fail-closed: reject if origin is missing or doesn't match expected origin exactly.

patterns.name: Phantom feature (silently blocked by rules)
patterns.description: Client-side Firestore write that hits `allow write: if false` security rule — the UI flow looks functional but every write silently fails at the database layer
patterns.detection: Map all client-side setDoc/updateDoc/deleteDoc/addDoc calls. Cross-reference collection paths with Firestore rules. Flag any hitting `if false` or missing write rules.
patterns.fix: Route writes through Cloud Functions with Admin SDK, or update Firestore rules to allow the specific write with proper constraints.

patterns.name: Missing server-side mediation for privileged writes
patterns.description: Client writes privileged fields (tier, points, role) directly to Firestore relying solely on security rules — no Cloud Function intermediary to validate business logic
patterns.detection: Search for updateDoc/setDoc calls that modify fields listed in 'isNotUpdatingPrivilegedFields()' rule. Flag any direct client writes to these fields.
patterns.fix: Create a Cloud Function endpoint that validates business logic before writing privileged fields. Remove direct client writes.

patterns.name: Dead dependency
patterns.description: Package listed in package.json dependencies but never imported in source code
patterns.detection: For each dep in package.json.dependencies, grep src/ for import/require of that package name. Zero hits = dead.
patterns.fix: Remove from package.json, run npm install

patterns.name: Shim/re-export indirection
patterns.description: File whose sole purpose is re-exporting from another module (backward-compat layer that adds confusion)
patterns.detection: File body is only import + re-export, no logic. Multiple files import from shim instead of canonical path.
patterns.fix: Migrate all consumers to canonical import path, delete shim file

patterns.name: Stub component with fake behavior
patterns.description: Component with setTimeout/fake submit, hardcoded dropdown options, and TODO comments — not connected to real data
patterns.detection: Check for: setTimeout in submit handlers, hardcoded <option> values, TODO comments in data flow paths
patterns.fix: Add STUB comment header for discoverability. Track as ticket for real implementation.

patterns.name: Ungated console.log in production
patterns.description: console.log calls in production code that leak internal state, user IDs, or debug info to browser console
patterns.detection: Grep for console.log in src/ (excluding test files). Flag any that log: success messages, user IDs/emails, internal counts, emoji-prefixed status messages
patterns.fix: Remove entirely (UI already shows feedback) or gate behind process.env.NODE_ENV === 'development'

patterns.name: Dead route/scaffolding page
patterns.description: Minimal page (e.g. 'Test Page Works') accessible in production with no auth guard, no metadata, no purpose
patterns.detection: Check for routes with <5 lines of code, no data fetching, no auth checks. Common names: /test, /debug, /demo
patterns.fix: Delete the page and its directory

patterns.name: Phantom client write (rules-blocked feature)
patterns.description: Client-side code writes to a Firestore/database path that is blocked by security rules (e.g. allow write: if false). The feature appears functional in the UI but every write silently fails. Often caused by security rules being tightened after the feature was built.
patterns.detection: Map all client-side write operations (setDoc, updateDoc, deleteDoc, .rpc()). Cross-reference each target path against database rules/RLS policies. If the rule blocks the write, it's a phantom feature.
patterns.fix: Either: (a) move the write to a server-side function (Cloud Function / API route) that uses Admin SDK, or (b) update security rules to allow the specific write pattern. Never leave dead writes in production.

patterns.name: Untyped Supabase response
patterns.description: Supabase .from().select() join query result cast to `any` instead of typed interface, losing type safety on nested relations
patterns.detection: Grep for `as any` or `: any` near .from( or .select( calls. Check for explicit `any` on data destructuring from Supabase queries.
patterns.fix: Create typed interface matching the Supabase join shape and cast data to it

patterns.name: Unused imports/parameters (strict tsconfig)
patterns.description: Unused imports, variables, or function parameters not caught because noUnusedLocals/noUnusedParameters are disabled in tsconfig.json
patterns.detection: Check tsconfig.json for noUnusedLocals and noUnusedParameters flags. If missing, enable and rebuild to surface issues.
patterns.fix: Add flags to tsconfig.json strict section. Fix surfaced errors (remove unused imports, prefix unused params with _).

patterns.name: Cached Firebase token on auth gate
patterns.description: Firebase getIdTokenResult() called without `true` parameter on auth-gate paths — returns cached token that can be up to 1 hour stale, allowing revoked users to retain access
patterns.detection: Grep for getIdTokenResult() without `true` argument. Flag any on security-critical paths (role checks, admin gates, permission bypasses).
patterns.fix: Change to getIdTokenResult(true) to force token refresh and get current claims from server

patterns.name: Oversized monolith component
patterns.description: Single file exceeding 400 lines that mixes data fetching, business logic, and rendering — violates single responsibility principle
patterns.detection: Count lines per file in src/. Flag any .tsx/.ts file >400 lines. Prioritize files >800 lines.
patterns.fix: Extract into custom hooks (useXxx), sub-components, and utility functions. Target <400 lines per file.

patterns.name: Icon-only button without aria-label
patterns.description: Button element contains only an icon (SVG, emoji, or symbol character like ✕) with no visible text — screen readers announce it as just 'button' with no context, violating WCAG 2.1 SC 4.1.2 (Name, Role, Value). Also breaks voice navigation (users cannot say 'click [name]') and makes test selectors fragile.
patterns.detection: Find all <button> elements. Check if the button's children contain visible text or an aria-label/aria-labelledby attribute. Flag any button whose only children are <svg>, emoji characters, or symbol entities without an accessible name.
patterns.fix: Add aria-label describing the action (not the icon). Use imperative verbs: 'Close modal', 'Dismiss alert', 'Remove item' — never 'X button' or 'Cross icon'. If the button already has visible text, do NOT add aria-label (the text is the accessible name).

patterns.name: API route without test coverage
patterns.description: POST/PUT/DELETE API route handler with auth, CSRF, and validation logic but zero test coverage — regression risk on security-critical code paths
patterns.detection: List all files matching app/api/*/route.ts. Cross-reference with __tests__/ directories. Flag any route without a corresponding test file.
patterns.fix: Write tests calling the route handler directly with mocked Supabase clients. Cover: CSRF rejection, auth checks, validation, happy path, error paths.

patterns.name: Timer/interval hook without fake timer tests
patterns.description: React hook using setTimeout/setInterval/Date.now for session timeouts or polling without tests using vi.useFakeTimers — behavior untested at time boundaries
patterns.detection: Grep for setTimeout|setInterval|Date.now in hooks/. Check if corresponding __tests__/ file exists with vi.useFakeTimers.
patterns.fix: Write tests with vi.useFakeTimers() + vi.advanceTimersByTime(). Test: warning threshold, timeout callback, reset behavior, cleanup on unmount.

patterns.name: Missing React error boundary
patterns.description: Application has no React error boundaries — any unhandled render error kills the entire component tree with a blank white screen and no recovery option
patterns.detection: Search for ErrorBoundary, componentDidCatch, getDerivedStateFromError, or error.tsx files. If none found, the app has no crash containment.
patterns.fix: Add at minimum a root error boundary in layout.tsx. For complex apps, add nested boundaries around major sections (admin panel, dashboard, forms).

patterns.name: Silent data suppression (unchecked .error on query result)
patterns.description: Supabase/Firebase query result destructured for .data only, without checking .error — a failed query silently leaves state at [] and UI shows zero values instead of an error state
patterns.detection: Grep for `.from(` or `supabase.` calls. Check if the response destructures both { data, error } and handles the error case. Flag any that only check `if (data)`.
patterns.fix: Always destructure { data, error }. If error, show user-facing error state (toast, banner, or inline message). Never render zero values as a proxy for failed fetches.

patterns.name: Decorative UI control (no onChange/no state)
patterns.description: Interactive UI element (dropdown, toggle, date picker) that appears functional but has no onChange handler, no state binding, and no effect on displayed data
patterns.detection: Search for <select>, <input type='checkbox'>, or toggle components. Check if they have onChange, value/checked props, and a connected state variable that affects data fetching.
patterns.fix: Either wire the control to state and data fetching, or remove it. Decorative controls erode user trust.

patterns.name: onSnapshot listener without error callback
patterns.description: Firestore onSnapshot() with only a success callback — if the query fails (permissions, network), the listener silently stops updating and the UI shows stale or empty data with no indication
patterns.detection: Grep for onSnapshot( calls. Check if each has a second argument (error callback) or uses the { next, error } object form. Flag any with only one callback.
patterns.fix: Add error callback as second argument: (error) => { console.error(...); setError(true); }. Show a user-facing error banner when the listener fails.
